abstract: The component for setting apart content that is not directly related to the article, such as promotional content.
overview:
## Discussion

Use an `aside` component to hold information not directly related to your article. Use an `aside` to help Siri make the most informed content recommendations to people using Apple News and to provide better accuracy for placing your content within feeds, such as the Today feed, channel feeds, and topic feeds.

When News recommends content, it ignores any content in the `aside` component, so you can put content there that is not directly related to the article, such as promotional content, links to other articles, or author biographies.

### Example
rawMarkdown: --- source: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/Aside crawled: 2026-03-10T14:19:35Z --- # Aside **Object** The component for setting apart content that is not directly related to the article, such as promotional content. ## Declaration ```swift object Aside ``` ## Discussion Use an `aside` component to hold information not directly related to your article. Use an `aside` to help Siri make the most informed content recommendations to people using Apple News and to provide better accuracy for placing your content within feeds, such as the Today feed, channel feeds, and topic feeds. When News recommends content, it ignores any content in the `aside` component, so you can put content there that is not directly related to the article, such as promotional content, links to other articles, or author biographies. ### Example ```json { "components": [ { "role": "aside", "components": [ { "role": "body", "text": "Swift Playgrounds expands coding education to robots, drones and musical instruments", "format": "html" } ] } ] } ``` ## Related Documentation - **About Containers**: Learn the basic Apple News Format container concepts required for the three advanced tutorials. ## Article Structure - **Nesting Components in an Article**: Use container components to create the component hierarchies you need for special article designs. - **Adding a Scene to a Chapter or a Section Header**: Add a scene to your article to create special effects. - **Creating an Article Link**: Link to an article by using the article-linking container component. - **Displaying Components Side By Side**: Configure a side-by-side, horizontal arrangement of components for your article. - **Header**: The component for defining the top area of an article, chapter, or section. - **Container**: Properties shared by all container types. - **Section**: The component for organizing an article into sections. - **Chapter**: The component for organizing an article into chapters. - **CollectionDisplay**: An object used in any container component type to define how the collection of child components is presented. - **HorizontalStackDisplay**: The object for displaying components side by side in a Container component. - **FlexibleSpacer**: The component for redistributing empty space inside a horizontal stack collection. - **Divider**: The component for defining a horizontal line to visually divide parts of your article. - **ArticleLink**: The container component for creating a link to an article. - **SupportedArticleIdentifier**: The patterns supported for article identifiers in UUID format. - **PublisherArticleIdentifier**: The identifier provided by the publisher. ## Inherits From - Container
